title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Frizzy Hair
intro: Herbal teas for Frizzy hair are a natural remedy that uses various herbs to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine. These teas work by providing essential nutrients, antioxidants, and humectant properties that help to lock in moisture, smooth the cuticle, and reduce protein loss, thereby treating frizzy hair. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat frizzy hair include rosemary tea, which stimulates blood flow to the scalp, lavender tea, which calms and soothes the hair, hibiscus tea, which adds shine and reduces frizz, green tea, which provides antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile tea, which soothes and calms the hair, and aloe vera tea, which moisturizes and hydrates the hair. By incorporating these herbal teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can achieve healthier, shinier, and more manageable hair with reduced frizz.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair due to its properties that help to smooth and tame unruly locks. The herbal preparation's ability to soothe and calm the scalp helps to reduce frizz by promoting a healthy scalp environment, while its moisturizing properties hydrate the hair strands, leaving them soft and manageable. The bioactive constituents of Cymbopogon citratus, including citral and geraniol, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to repair damaged hair and prevent breakage, further contributing to a smooth and frizz-free hair texture. Regular consumption of lemongrass tea has been found to provide numerous benefits, including reduced frizz, improved hair elasticity, and enhanced overall hair health, making it a popular natural remedy for frizzy hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a separate container and let it cool.
Once cooled, add 1 tablespoon of lemon juice to the tea and mix well.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help reduce frizz.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can help to treat frizzy hair by controlling oil production and balancing the scalp's pH levels, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. To use Cymbopogon citratus teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts, patch test on a small area of skin, and avoid using it on sensitive areas or if you have a history of skin conditions.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo and conditioner as usual
Steep 1 tablespoon of Cymbopogon citratus tea in 1 cup of hot water for 5 minutes
Let the tea cool down to room temperature
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing 2-3 times a week
Leave the tea on hair for 5-10 minutes before rinsing with cold water

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to treat frizzy hair, a common issue caused by dryness and damage to the hair shaft. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including camphor, borneol, and rosmarinic acid, work together to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and adding shine. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat frizzy hair include improved manageability, reduced flyaways, and a softer, more vibrant appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer.
Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the tea and mix well.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be an effective treatment for frizzy hair, but potential side effects may include digestive issues due to the high concentration of essential oils, as well as skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, take a small amount to start, mix with a carrier oil before applying to the hair, and avoid using it on damaged or sensitive scalp areas.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a gentle shampoo and towel dry.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis in 1 cup of boiling water.
Allow the tea to cool for 30 minutes before use.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing, 2-3 times a week.
Leave the tea on for 5-10 minutes, then shampoo and condition as usual.

preparation_amazon.title: Palm Beach Medicinal Herbs - Rosemary Tea - Pure Herbal Tea Series, 30ct

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been found to be beneficial in treating frizzy hair due to their ability to help balance the scalp's pH levels, reduce inflammation, and improve blood circulation, which in turn promotes healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including linalool and linalyl acetate, possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe the scalp and reduce frizz. By consuming these teas, individuals can experience the benefits of reduced frizz, improved hair texture, and enhanced shine, all of which contribute to healthier and more manageable hair. Overall, the use of Lavandula angustifolia teas can be a natural and effective way to address frizzy hair issues and promote overall hair well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
Repeat the process 1-2 times a week for best results in reducing frizz.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to help treat frizzy hair by balancing the scalp's natural pH and reducing inflammation, but it may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interact with certain medications. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is essential to patch test the tea on a small area of skin before applying it to the scalp and to avoid using it if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergic reactions.
preparation_usage: Mix 2 tablespoons of Lavandula angustifolia tea with 1 cup of water.
Use this mixture as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
Leave the mixture on your hair for 5-10 minutes.
Rinse your hair with cold water to seal the cuticles.
Use this treatment 1-2 times a week for best results.

preparation_amazon.title: Tiesta Tea - Lavender Chamomile Herbal Tea | Loose Leaf | Calming Blend with Chamomile and Lavender | Caffeine-Free Herbal | Great for Hot or Iced Brews | Resealable Bulk Pouch, 200 Cups | 8 Ounce

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair, a common issue caused by dryness and damage to the hair shaft. The properties of Aloe barbadensis teas, including its soothing, moisturizing, and anti-inflammatory effects, help to treat frizzy hair by hydrating the hair and scalp, reducing inflammation, and promoting a healthy hair growth environment. The bioactive constituents of Aloe barbadensis teas, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E, help to repair and nourish the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways. Regular use of Aloe barbadensis teas can provide numerous benefits, including improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and enhanced shine, making it an effective natural remedy for frizzy hair treatment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel.
Heat the water in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the Aloe barbadensis gel to the water and stir well.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before applying it to your frizzy hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be an effective remedy for treating frizzy hair, but it may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly if they have sensitive skin. To minimize potential side effects, it's recommended to do a patch test before using the tea as a hair treatment and to avoid applying it to broken or irritated skin.
preparation_usage: Mix 1/4 cup of Aloe barbadensis tea with 1 cup of water.
Apply the mixture to your hair, from roots to ends.
Leave it on for 15-20 minutes, then rinse with shampoo.
Repeat usage 1-2 times a week for best results.
Use 1-2 cups of Aloe barbadensis tea for long hair, 1 cup for short hair.

preparation_amazon.title: The Republic of Tea Beautifying Botanicals® Clean Beauty Berry Aloe Herbal Tea Bags (36 count)

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm teas, have been used to treat frizzy hair ailments due to their soothing and calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat frizzy hair by smoothing the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and flyaways, and adding shine to the hair. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis teas, such as rosmarinic acid and luteolin, help to treat frizzy hair by inhibiting the production of inflammatory cytokines and promoting hair growth. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat frizzy hair include reduced frizz and flyaways, improved hair texture, and enhanced shine, making it a popular natural remedy for hair care.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, dried or fresh.
Steep 1 tablespoon of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool down.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing and conditioning your hair.
Repeat the process once a week for best results, as frizzy hair can be a long-term issue.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: N/A
preparation_usage: Add 1 teaspoon of Melissa officinalis tea to 1 cup of water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing once a week.
Leave the tea on your hair for 2-3 minutes before rinsing.
Use 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea as a hair mask once a month.

preparation_amazon.title: Maxx Herb Lemon Balm Extract - Max Strength Liquid Tincture Absorbs Better Than Capsules or Tea, for Nervous System Support, & Stress Management - Alcohol Free - 4 Oz Bottle (60 Servings)

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, derived from the herb thyme, have been used to treat frizzy hair due to its properties as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing the damage caused by free radicals and soothing the scalp, thereby reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. The bioactive constituents of Thymus vulgaris teas, including thymol and carvacrol, have been found to have hair-smoothing properties, helping to tame frizzy hair and leave it looking healthy and manageable. The benefits of this herbal preparation include a reduction in frizz, improved hair texture, and enhanced shine, making it a natural and effective solution for treating frizzy hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves, and a teapot.
Heat the water in the teapot until it boils.
Add the 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves to the boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Use the Thymus vulgaris tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help calm frizzy hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can potentially cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications when used to treat frizzy hair, particularly if consumed in high quantities or combined with other herbal remedies. To use Thymus vulgaris teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small amounts, avoiding contact with sensitive skin, and monitoring for any adverse reactions, and consider discontinuing use if symptoms persist or worsen.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo as usual.
Mix 10 drops of Thymus vulgaris essential oil with 1 cup of water.
Use the mixture as final rinse after shampooing.
Repeat 2-3 times a week for best results.
Leave the mixture on for 1-2 minutes before rinsing.

preparation_amazon.title: Buddha Teas Organic Thyme Leaf Tea - OU Kosher, USDA Organic, CCOF Organic, 18 Bleach-Free Tea Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas, also known as horsetail tea, have been used for centuries to treat frizzy hair due to its rich content of silica and other minerals. The high silica content in this herbal preparation helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair texture, reducing frizz and promoting smooth, healthy-looking hair. Bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids in Equisetum arvense teas also contribute to its hair-smoothing properties, helping to balance the scalp's natural pH and reduce hair frizz. By drinking Equisetum arvense teas regularly, individuals can enjoy the benefits of smoother, more manageable hair and a healthier scalp.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense plants or 2 tablespoons of dried plants.
Add 2 cups of boiling water to a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of the boiling water over the Equisetum arvense plants in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Equisetum arvense tea 1-2 times a week to help control frizzy hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat frizzy hair, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, or interactions with other medications, such as blood thinners or diabetes medications. When using Equisetum arvense teas to treat frizzy hair, take precautions to avoid consuming excessive amounts, as it can cause gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea, diarrhea, or stomach cramps, and to note that its effectiveness may vary depending on individual hair types and conditions.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Equisetum arvense in a cup
Steep in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing hair
Repeat usage 2-3 times a week for best results

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been studied for their potential benefits in treating frizzy hair, a common issue caused by dryness and damage to the hair shaft. The properties of Ginkgo biloba teas, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, help to treat this ailment by protecting the hair from damage and promoting healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work together to improve blood flow to the scalp, reduce oxidative stress, and strengthen hair follicles, ultimately leading to smoother and less frizzy hair. By incorporating Ginkgo biloba teas into your hair care routine, you may experience improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and a more manageable and healthy-looking mane.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Combine the Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture and discard the Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Cool the tea and use it as a final rinse after shampooing for frizzy hair control.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: N/A
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea daily for 2-3 weeks.
Start with 1 teaspoon of Ginkgo biloba powder per cup.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons after 1 week.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea 1-2 times a week for maintenance.
Comb and style hair as usual after tea consumption.

preparation_amazon.title: Tai Chi Think Sharp Energizing Tea (Ginseng Ginkgo Biloba) 12 Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas, derived from the rhizomes of the Curcuma longa plant, have been used to treat frizzy hair ailments due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat frizzy hair by reducing the growth of fungal pathogens that contribute to frizz, while also soothing and calming the scalp to minimize irritation. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin, turmerone, and atlantone, have been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines that can exacerbate frizzy hair. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat frizzy hair include improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and enhanced overall scalp health, making it a natural and effective remedy for those struggling with frizzy locks.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help reduce frizz.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to treat frizzy hair, but its use may lead to potential side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and stomach upset due to its high content of curcumin, a compound that can be irritating to some individuals. To minimize these risks, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to avoid using it on damaged or sensitive scalps, as well as to rinse the hair thoroughly after use to prevent any potential irritation.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a gentle shampoo.
Mix 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder with 1 cup of water.
Soak your hair in the mixture for 30 minutes.
Rinse your hair with lukewarm water and shampoo again.
Use this treatment once a week for 2 months.

preparation_amazon.title: Pure Ceylon Turmeric Brew - 100% Organic Ceylon Turmeric Tea Bags (40 Sachets - pack of 2 – 20 COUNT PER BOX)

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been used to treat frizzy hair due to their moisturizing and soothing properties. These properties help to treat frizzy hair by providing hydration to the hair strands and scalp, reducing dryness and brittleness. The bioactive constituents of ginger tea, including gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to smooth and calm the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and adding shine. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas has been shown to provide several benefits in treating frizzy hair, including improved hair elasticity, reduced hair breakage, and a smoother, more manageable texture.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/4 cup of fresh ginger root (Zingiber officinale) in a saucepan.
Peel and slice the ginger root into thin pieces.
Bring the water to a boil and add the sliced ginger. Reduce heat to medium-low.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup to remove the ginger pieces.
Drink the ginger tea while it's warm, ideally 1-2 cups per week to help reduce frizzy hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in treating frizzy hair, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, which may lead to sunburn or other skin issues. To minimize the risk of side effects, take precautions such as starting with small amounts, avoiding contact with the skin and eyes, and using a gentle shampoo and conditioner to maintain hair and scalp health.
preparation_usage: Use 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea as final rinse after shampooing hair.
Repeat usage 2 times a week for 6 weeks to achieve desired results.
Massage scalp gently for 5 minutes before rinsing with tea.
Leave tea on hair for 10-15 minutes before rinsing thoroughly with water.
Use 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale tea extract as hair serum for added benefits.

preparation_amazon.title: FGO Organic Ginger Tea, 100 Count, Eco-Conscious Tea Bags, Caffeine Free, Packaging May Vary (Pack of 1)

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair, a common condition characterized by unruly and unmanageable hair. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Silybum marianum teas help to treat frizzy hair by soothing the scalp, reducing inflammation, and protecting the hair from damage caused by free radicals. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, contribute to its hair-smoothing effects by strengthening hair follicles and promoting healthy hair growth. By incorporating Silybum marianum teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can enjoy the benefits of smoother, shinier, and more manageable hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of water.
Combine the flowers and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Let the liquid cool.
Once cooled, store the tea in a container and use 1-2 tablespoons as a hair treatment for frizzy hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat frizzy hair due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat frizzy hair, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, avoid consuming it with other medications or herbal supplements, and drink plenty of water to minimize potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo to remove dirt and oil.
Steep 1 tablespoon of Silybum marianum in 1 cup of hot water.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing, 2 times a week.
Leave the tea on hair for 30 minutes before rinsing with cold water.

preparation_amazon.title: Celebration Herbals Organic Milk Thistle Seed Herbal Tea -- 24 Tea Bags, NET WT.60, 2.11 OZ

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas, also known as nettle tea, have been used to treat the frizzy hair ailment due to their potential in smoothing and calming the hair follicles. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce frizz and add shine to the hair by protecting it from environmental stressors and damage. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica, including flavonoids and saponins, help to strengthen hair follicles and improve hair elasticity, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth. Regular consumption of Urtica dioica teas may also help to reduce dandruff and itchiness associated with frizzy hair, promoting a more manageable and healthy-looking hair texture.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Urtica dioica leaves.
Combine the leaves with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a week for frizzy hair treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can be used to treat frizzy hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or digestive issues due to the presence of urtic acid and other compounds. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, individuals should start with small amounts, monitor their body's response, and avoid applying the tea directly to the scalp or skin, as this may exacerbate the potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Urtica dioica root in a cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and steep the herb for 5-7 minutes.
Drink 1 cup of tea 2-3 times a week for frizzy hair.
Use 1 cup of tea as a final rinse after shampooing once a week.
Continue using tea for 6-8 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparation_amazon.title: The Republic of Tea — Organic Nettle SuperHerb Tea Tin, 36 Herbal Tea Bags, Naturally Caffeine-Free

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas have been used to treat frizzy hair due to its properties of soothing and calming the scalp, thereby reducing frizz and adding shine to hair. This herbal preparation helps to treat frizzy hair by regulating the moisture balance of the scalp and hair, resulting in smoother and more manageable locks. The bioactive constituents of Thymus serpyllum teas, including thymol and carvacrol, help to treat frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy hair growth. The benefits of using Thymus serpyllum teas to treat frizzy hair include improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and increased shine, making it an effective and natural remedy for this common hair ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Place the Thymus serpyllum leaves in the tea infuser or directly in the heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the leaves. Let the tea cool down to room temperature.
Use the cooled Thymus serpyllum tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help reduce frizz and add shine.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used as a potential treatment for frizzy hair, but it may cause some side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications. To use Thymus serpyllum teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it in excess, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.
preparation_usage: Wash hair with shampoo, then rinse thoroughly.
Mix 1-2 teaspoons of Thymus serpyllum tea with 1 cup of water.
Use as a final rinse after shampooing once a week.
Leave the tea mixture on hair for 5-10 minutes before rinsing.
Repeat the process for best results, ideally every 2-3 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair by reducing its unruliness and promoting a smooth, silky texture. The properties of this herbal preparation, including its high content of saponins and phenolic compounds, help to treat frizzy hair by reducing the cuticle's swelling and improving the hair's moisture balance. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa teas, such as avenanthramides and ferulic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect the hair from damage and promote its overall health. The benefits of using Avena sativa teas to treat frizzy hair include improved manageability, reduced frizz, and a more radiant and healthy appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw) and 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the Avena sativa in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea once a day to help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and smoothing the hair cuticle, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications. When using Avena sativa teas to treat frizzy hair, take precautions by starting with a small dose, avoiding contact with sensitive skin, and monitoring for any adverse reactions, and also be aware that Avena sativa may exacerbate conditions such as thyroid problems.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair due to their hydrating and nourishing properties that help to lock in moisture and reduce frizz. The tea's active compounds, including cineole and terpinen-4-ol, exhibit antiseptic and antifungal properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy hair growth environment. Regular consumption of Melaleuca alternifolia tea may also help to strengthen hair follicles, improve hair texture, and reduce split ends, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair. By incorporating Melaleuca alternifolia tea into one's hair care routine, individuals can benefit from its natural and chemical-free approach to hair care, promoting healthy and beautiful-looking hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of the Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the cooled water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help reduce frizz and add shine to your hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat frizzy hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or an increase in oil production on the scalp, which can potentially worsen frizzy hair. Precautions include avoiding excessive consumption, as it may lead to an imbalance in the scalp's natural pH levels, and using a small amount initially to test for individual tolerance and sensitivity.

preparations.herb_desc: Ocimum basilicum teas, derived from the leaves of sweet basil, have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair by reducing its unruly texture and promoting smoothness. The herbal preparation exhibits anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, thereby reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents present in Ocimum basilicum teas, including eugenol, linalool, and beta-caryophyllene, are believed to inhibit the production of stress hormones that contribute to frizzy hair, while also enhancing the hair's natural moisture balance. Regular consumption of Ocimum basilicum teas can lead to benefits such as reduced frizz, improved hair elasticity, and enhanced overall hair health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/4 cup of fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves.
Boil the water in a pot and add the Ocimum basilicum leaves.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool down. Use 1/4 cup of the cooled mixture as the tea.
Apply the tea to your frizzy hair by massaging it into the scalp and roots, then rinse with warm water.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ocimum basilicum teas can be beneficial in treating frizzy hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications, particularly those affecting blood pressure or heart rate. To safely use Ocimum basilicum teas for this purpose, it's essential to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dosage, avoid consuming it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and monitor your body's response to the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Santalum album teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair, a common issue caused by excessive moisture and damaged hair cuticles. This herbal preparation helps to treat frizzy hair by its antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which reduce dandruff and itchiness, and its astringent properties, which help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce oil production. The bioactive constituents of Santalum album, including santalol and santalenes, help to treat frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and repairing damaged hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz. The benefits of using Santalum album teas to treat frizzy hair include improved hair texture, reduced frizz and flyaways, and a healthy-looking scalp.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Santalum album leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 10-15 minutes to make a strong tea.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool down.
Once cooled, use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
Leave the tea on your hair for 5-10 minutes before washing it out with cold water.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Santalum album teas can be used as a potential treatment for frizzy hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of scalp itchiness and dryness due to its essential oils. When using Santalum album teas to treat frizzy hair, it is recommended to perform a patch test on a small area of skin, use a diluted solution, and avoid applying the tea directly to the scalp, as this may lead to further irritation.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been used to treat frizzy hair by its ability to balance scalp pH and reduce frizz. The antifungal and antibacterial properties of Camellia sinensis teas help to control scalp infections and promote a healthy scalp environment, which in turn helps to reduce frizz. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and amino acids, help to nourish and protect the hair follicles, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair. Regular use of Camellia sinensis teas can also help to reduce hair breakage, promote hair growth, and leave hair with a healthy shine, making it an effective and natural remedy for frizzy hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 3-5 minutes. Then, strain the mixture.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the tea with 1 cup of cold water. Let it cool.
Wet your hair with the cooled tea mixture. Massage it into your scalp.
Rinse your hair with warm water and shampoo as usual. Repeat this process once a week.
preparations.preparation_study: According to International journal of molecular sciences, Camellia sinensis teas may not be directly related to treating frizzy hair, but it may have beneficial features in repairing or recovering from hair follicle disruption due to its flavonoids, which could potentially aid in reducing hair loss and stimulating new hair growth.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can help alleviate frizzy hair by providing antioxidants and improving hair texture, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interaction with certain medications. To use Camellia sinensis teas safely, be cautious when applying it to sensitive skin, avoid ingesting large quantities, and start with a small patch test to assess individual tolerance.

preparations.herb_desc: Centella asiatica teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair, a common issue caused by dryness and lack of moisture. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Centella asiatica teas help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy hair growth environment. The bioactive constituents such as asiatic acid, madecassic acid, and brahmoside in Centella asiatica teas work synergistically to provide hydration, improve elasticity, and reduce frizz, resulting in smoother and more manageable hair. Regular consumption of Centella asiatica teas can help to improve the overall health of your hair, reduce frizz, and leave your locks looking soft, silky, and vibrant.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Centella asiatica leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Rinse the leaves and place them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool. Use 1/4 cup of the tea as a hair treatment, applied from roots to ends, and leave it on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Centella asiatica teas for frizzy hair is not mentioned in the provided study, as the study focuses on the documentation of herbal tea plant species used for health and detoxification purposes, but it does not provide information on its use for hair care or frizzy hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Centella asiatica teas can be beneficial in treating frizzy hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation or allergic reactions, particularly if consumed in large quantities or used on sensitive skin.

To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase as needed, avoid using the tea on broken or irritated skin, and be cautious when consuming it for extended periods, as excessive use may lead to stomach upset or interact with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas have been traditionally used to treat frizzy hair, a common ailment characterized by unmanageable, curly, or wavy hair. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Cassia auriculata teas help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. The bioactive constituents present in Cassia auriculata teas, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to repair and protect the hair follicles, promoting healthy and manageable hair. The regular consumption of Cassia auriculata teas has been found to improve hair texture and reduce frizz, making it an effective natural remedy for treating this common hair concern.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cassia auriculata flowers and 2 cups of water in a pot.
Boil the water and add the Cassia auriculata flowers. Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool. Discard the flowers.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair. Pour the tea over your hair and leave it on for 5-10 minutes.
Rinse your hair thoroughly with cold water and style as usual.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can help to reduce frizzy hair to some extent; however, its use may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of thyroid problems due to its high iodine content. Precautions to take when using Cassia auriculata teas include monitoring your thyroid function regularly, avoiding its use in individuals with pre-existing thyroid conditions, and starting with small dosages to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.
